candidate_problem_record.subject: physics
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: The one-dimensional quantum mechanical potential energy of a particle of mass $m$ is given by $$ V(x)=\begin{cases} V_{0}\delta(x), & -a<x<\infty \\ \infty, & x<-a \end{cases} $$ as shown in Fig. 1.19. At time $t=0$, the wave function of the particle is completely confined to the region $-a<x<0$. [Define the quantities $k=\sqrt{2 m E}/\hbar$ and $\alpha=2 m V_{0}/\hbar^{2}$] Find the (real) solutions for the energy eigenfunctions in the two regions (up to an overall constant) which satisfy the boundary conditions.
candidate_problem_record.raw_answer_text: $\psi_{k}(x)=\begin{cases} c_{k}\sin k(x+a), & (-a<x<0)\\ c_{k}\sin k(x+a)+A_{k}\sin kx, & (x\ge0)\\ 0, & (x<-a) \end{cases},\quad A_{k}=\frac{c_{k}\alpha}{k}\sin ka,\quad c_{k}=\left\{\frac{\pi}{2}\Bigl[1+\frac{\alpha\sin2ka}{k}+\Bigl(\frac{\alpha\sin ka}{k}\Bigr)^2\Bigr]\right\}^{-\frac{1}{2}}$
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: images field contains a PIL image object representation rather than a file path or URL
